solutions.solution:
ii) two, where the present situation talks of a simple fact (not a statement) and the suggested course of action suggests of an improvement. Therefore, in solving questions of this type your ultimate aim should be to be first see:

You must have understood the first step clearly.
It is:
First step: Decide quickly whether the problem involves a problem-solution relationship or a fact-improvement relationship.

Now that you have decided the type of the problem, you can go through the logical steps for each one of these is outlined below.

Type I: Problem-Solution Relationship
As already stated, in this type of questions, the suggestion gives a solution while the given fact is a type of problem. It is easy to check if the solution or course of action, in these cases, is suitable, and when it is suggested course of action suitably, the suggested course of action can be declared suitable if:

i) It does solve or reduce or minimise the problem;
and
ii) It does not escalate the solution.

Now, by your everyday experiences, your common sense and general knowledge, you can easily judge whether a suggested course of action solves (or reduces or minimises) a problem. That is not difficult to guess.

Then before proceeding ahead, for that, you should check if the solution is applicable in everyday life – it must be practical and wise to follow it; we should have understood these points clearly, let us go over them in detail, one by one.

Evaluating Given Courses of Action
For statement, it is a biologically established fact that malaria can be prevented by using safeguards against mosquito bites; it is a socially established fact that criminals should not be treated as punishable wretches but they should be treated as criminals because their acts are anti-social, harmless, etc. Now, compare these with the following:

Explanation: As discussed earlier, Anti-mosquito liquids should be sprayed in northern Rajasthan. (✓)

Ex 2: Statement: A child was caught in a train while attempting to steal the jewellery of a respectable lady.

Courses of action: I. The child should be severely beaten. (×)
II. The child should be handed over to child welfare society. (✓)

[The inappropriate course of action has been attached a (×) mark while the appropriate one a (✓) mark.]

In Expert's predict us: In some cases, the problem that is talked about may be relatively simple, too. It will not be completely new but it will not be very old either; therefore, the solution cannot be declared as a universal fact. However, here, based on previous experiences, in the same or similar type of fact situation, it can be easily ascertained that the problem is indeed solved by this particular solution.

Similarly, past experiences tell us that India has fallen victim to foreign powers; why when it has not remained united. Past experiences also tell us that the dowry problem has not been solved despite several legislations made against it. [Note that we are talking of experience and not established fact. It is not an established fact that whenever India develops the situation, foreign powers will come to rule us; nor is it an established fact that legislations cannot control the dowry problem; maybe a tougher legislation will; but till now, our experiences have proved otherwise.] Now, compare these with the following:

Ex 3: Statement: India is gravely endangered by several expansionist foreign powers.

Course of action: Efforts should be made that the Indians remain united for any eventuality. (✓)

Ex 4: Statement: Casteism has become an integral part of our life, to an alarming extent.

Course of action: Strict legislation should be passed promoting inter-caste marriages. (×)

Explanation: Experiences tell us that legislation could do nothing in the case of dowry. Casteism is equally deep rooted in our minds as dowry is. So, if legislation failed in the case of dowry, how could it be expected to succeed in the case of inter-caste marriages?
